revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:福島わらじまつり]]
{{otheruses|A summer festival held in August|A religious event held during the Lunar New Year in February|{{ill|Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi|ja|信夫三山暁まいり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi|qq=}}}} '''Fukushima Waraji Festival''' (福島わらじまつり) is a [[Summer Matsuri|Summer festival]] held in {{ill|Fukushima (city)|en|Fukushima (city)|qid=Q161176|lt=Fukushima City|1=Fukushima (city)}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|qid=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.
The festival traces its roots to the winter religious event {{ill|Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi|ja|信夫三山暁まいり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi|qq=}}, which has been held since the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. However, the Waraji Festival was established as a separate summer event in 1970 (Shōwa 45).<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.tohokukanko.jp/attractions/detail_1008889.html |title=Fukushima Waraji Festival |access-date=2024-02-17 |publisher={{ill|Tohoku Tourism Promotion Organization|ja|東北観光推進機構|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tohoku Tourism Promotion Organization|1=Tohoku Tourism Promotion Organization|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97169399}}}}</ref> It is held annually in early August and features the carrying of a giant {{ill|waraji|ja|わらじ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/waraji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 草鞋|1=Waraji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q696696}}, said to be the largest in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The large waraji offered at {{ill|Haguro Shrine (Fukushima)|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine (Fukushima)|1=Haguro Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11610434}} is paired with the waraji from the {{ill|Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi|ja|信夫三山暁まいり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi|qq=}} in February to form a complete pair, symbolizing prayers for strong and healthy legs.
Though the festival was one of {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|qid=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}'s representative celebrations, it was overshadowed by the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Tohoku|lt=Three Great Festivals of Tohoku|lt=Three Great Festivals of Tohoku|ja|東北三大祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Tohoku|qq=}} and remained relatively unknown outside the region. However, after the {{ill|Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|en|Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|qid=Q1136168|lt=Great East Japan Earthquake|1=Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}}, it was included in the {{ill|Tohoku Rokkonsai (Six Souls Festival)|ja|東北六魂祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tohoku Rokkonsai (Six Souls Festival)|1=Tohoku Rokkonsai Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11526142}}, and has since been recognized as one of the {{ill|Six Great Festivals of Tohoku|ja|東北6大祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Great Festivals of Tohoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東北六魂祭|1=Tohoku Rokkonsai Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11526142}}.
== Overview ==
The festival is held annually around {{ill|Fukushima Station|en|Fukushima Station|qid=Q416034|lt=Fukushima Station|1=Fukushima Station}} East Exit, from the first Friday to Sunday of August.<ref name="waraji">{{Cite web|url=https://www.waraji.co.jp/|title=Fukushima Waraji Festival|publisher=Fukushima Waraji Festival Executive Committee|accessdate=2021-01-19}}</ref>
The festival, which was started in 1970 (Shōwa 45) by {{ill|Fukushima (city)|en|Fukushima (city)|qid=Q161176|lt=Fukushima City|1=Fukushima (city)}} and the {{ill|Fukushima Chamber of Commerce and Industry|ja|福島商工会議所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Chamber of Commerce and Industry|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, underwent a major renewal in 2019 to mark its 50th anniversary.<ref name="FukushimaCity2018120301">{{Cite conference|title=Fukushima City Council|conference=Heisei 30 December Regular Meeting - December 3rd - Session 1|quote=Upgrading the 50th Fukushima Waraji Festival into a newly renewed event|date=2018-12-03}}</ref> The renewal was supervised by Fukushima-native musician {{ill|Otomo Yoshihide|en|Otomo Yoshihide|qid=Q1969558|lt=Otomo Yoshihide|1=Otomo Yoshihide}}.<ref name="FukushimaCity2019031407">{{Cite conference|title=Fukushima City Council|conference=Heisei 31 March Regular Meeting - March 14th - Session 7|quote=We requested overall production by Fukushima-connected musician Otomo Yoshihide, revamping the dances, music, and the festival structure.|date=2019-03-14}}</ref>
The festival now incorporates reimagined Fukushima folklore,<ref name="waraji_special">{{Cite web|url=https://www.waraji.co.jp/special/|title=Special Roundtable: The Meaning of Reforming the Fukushima Waraji Festival|publisher=Fukushima Waraji Festival Executive Committee|accessdate=2021-01-19}}</ref> with new dances and costumes. The previously prerecorded music was replaced with live performances featuring flutes and drums.<ref name="waraji_special" /><ref name="FukushimaCity2019031801">{{Cite conference|title=Fukushima City Council|conference=Heisei 31 March 18th Budget Special Committee Economic Welfare Section - March 18th - Session 1|quote=Using drums and other instruments for the performance.|date=2019-03-18}}</ref>
The main events include: * '''Waraji Dance''': A parade featuring "Heisei Waraji Ondo" and a flowing dance inspired by Benkei and Ushiwakamaru. * '''Dancing Soda Night''': A freestyle dance event performed to "Dancing Soda Night" by the Warajis. * '''Waraji Race''': A race with different categories for elementary school students, women, and general participants.
On the first day, the festival features '''Waraji Dance Part I and II''', along with '''Dancing Soda Night Part I'''. The second day includes the '''Waraji Race''' (for all categories) and '''Dancing Soda Night Part II'''. Winners receive cash prizes. In recent years, singers known as "Warasingers" have been recruited through auditions to perform the Waraji Ondo.
On the third day, waraji sandals and '''ema''' (votive plaques) are dedicated at {{ill|Haguro Shrine (Fukushima)|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine (Fukushima)|1=Haguro Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11610434}}, and the waraji sandals are hung on a tower alongside those from the {{ill|Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi|ja|信夫三山暁まいり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi|qq=}} festival (held on the 14th day of the Lunar New Year).
The first edition of the renewed festival, the '''50th Fukushima Waraji Festival in 2019''', featured "Heisei Waraji Festival Final" on August 2nd and "New Waraji Festival" on August 3rd.<ref name="50th">{{Cite web|publisher=Fukushima City Tourism Note|title=50th Fukushima Waraji Festival|url=https://www.f-kankou.jp/event/pickup/925|accessdate=2021-01-19}}</ref>
=== Waraji Dance ===
This event takes place on the first day. Participants parade down {{ill|Japan National Route 13|en|Japan National Route 13|qid=Q1056193|lt=National Route 13 (Japan)|1=Japan National Route 13}} while dancing to the "Waraji Ondo," a traditional '''bon-odori''' style song. The choreography is predetermined and has a relaxed, flowing feel. General participants cannot join the event freely, but since '''2007''', they have been allowed to join for the final turn.
=== Dancing Soda Night ===
A reimagined version of the former "Peach Samba" event, now held over two days. The dance is performed to a rap-style remix of the "Waraji Ondo," with no fixed choreography. The second session (Part II) on the final day features an extended parade route.
Only pre-registered groups may participate, but some teams allow outsiders to join. Three categories offer a '''cash prize of 200,000 yen each'''. Until '''2006''', results were updated in real time at the venue and in newspapers. Additionally, there was an online voting system called the "Waraji Street Award," which awarded '''10,000 yen''' to the winner.
=== Waraji Race ===
Participants compete in pulling '''waraji sandals''' or '''floats''' over a set distance. * Elementary school students and women pull '''small waraji'''. * The '''general category''' originally involved pulling festival floats but was '''changed in 2016''' to a '''race carrying a 5-meter-long waraji'''. * In the preliminary round, two teams race against each other, trying to grab either the '''gold''' or '''silver''' waraji first. The winners proceed to the finals.
The '''competition waraji''' is displayed inside the '''Shinkansen gate''' at '''Fukushima Station West Exit'''.
=== Festival Plaza ===
A side event area located at '''Machinaka Plaza'''. The venue hosts '''street musician performances''', special corners by '''Fukushima University''' students, and '''live performances by Hayata Akashi''', the arranger of Dancing Soda Night.
== Thematic Songs ==
* 1970: "Waraji Ondo" (Lyrics: Hiroya Mogi; Music: {{ill|Yūji Koseki|en|Yūji Koseki|qid=Q3574303|lt=Yuji Koseki|1=Yūji Koseki}}; Vocals: {{ill|Kazuo Funaki|en|Kazuo Funaki|qid=Q1075063|lt=Kazuo Funaki|1=Kazuo Funaki}}) * 1999: "Dancing Soda Night" (Original Lyrics: Hiroya Mogi, Toshio Oka; Arranged by Hayata Akashi; Rap: Hayata Akashi & Brian Peck; Vocals: Warajis)
== Management and Broadcast ==
The festival is organized by the Fukushima Waraji Festival Executive Committee, composed of the {{ill|Fukushima Chamber of Commerce and Industry|ja|福島商工会議所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Chamber of Commerce and Industry|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Fukushima Youth Association|ja|福島青年会議所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Youth Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and other local organizations.
The event is broadcast by local Fukushima-based stations, including {{ill|Fukushima Television|en|Fukushima Television|qid=Q8014010|lt=Fukushima TV|1=Fukushima Television}}, {{ill|TV-U Fukushima|en|TV-U Fukushima|qid=Q8014805|lt=TV-U Fukushima|1=TV-U Fukushima}}, and {{ill|Radio Fukushima|en|Radio Fukushima|qid=Q11236472|lt=Radio Fukushima|1=Radio Fukushima}}.
=== Response to COVID-19 === In 2020, the festival was canceled for the first time due to the {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019th year -)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20200501-OFSHDH7P6FP2XMDRVCJACIZLXU/|title=Fukushima Waraji Festival Canceled for the First Time Since 1970|newspaper=Sankei News|date=2020-05-01}}</ref> To maintain the festival’s spirit, a large waraji was displayed in front of Fukushima Station on August 8, accompanied by a traditional plague-prevention prayer.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Record Photos of the 2020 Plague-Prevention Prayer Ceremony|publisher=Fukushima Waraji Festival Executive Committee|url=https://www.waraji.co.jp/topics/251/|date=2020-08-19|accessdate=2021-01-17}}</ref>
== External Links ==
* [http://www.fmcnet.co.jp/waraji Fukushima Waraji Festival Official Site]

{{ill|Tokyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Fukagawa Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukagawa Matsuri|qq=}} – One of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Edo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Edo|qq=}}. * {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Prefecture|qq=}} * {{ill|Iida-machi Toro-yama Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iida-machi Toro-yama Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}} * {{ill|Gujo Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Tsushima Tennō Festival|qq=}} – One of the {{ill|Three Great River Festivals of Japan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great River Festivals of Japan|qq=}}. * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Matsuri|qq=}} – One of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Japan|qq=}}. * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Tenjin Festival|ja|湯村温泉 (兵庫県)#イベント|ja|湯村温泉 (兵庫県)#イベント|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q8061163}} * {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Prefecture|qq=}} * {{ill|Tottori Shan-Shan Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Shan-Shan Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture|qq=}} * {{ill|Awa Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Odori|qq=}} – Its {{ill|Tokushima City Awa Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City Awa Odori|qq=}} is one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Shikoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Shikoku|qq=}}. * {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kochi Prefecture|qq=}} * {{ill|YOSAKOI Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/YOSAKOI Festival|qq=}} – One of the Three Great Festivals of Shikoku. * {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}} * {{ill|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata Gion Yamakasa|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumamoto Prefecture|qq=}} * {{ill|Yamaga Lantern Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Lantern Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|qid=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}} * {{ill|Miyakonojo Rokugatsudo Okage Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyakonojo Rokugatsudo Okage Festival|qq=}}
* {{ill|Sabantuy|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabantuy|qq=}} – A summer festival held along the middle reaches of the {{ill|Volga River|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Volga River|qq=}} in {{ill|Russia|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Russia|qq=}}. * {{ill|Spring Matsuri|lt=Spring Matsuri|lt=Spring Festivals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Autumn Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Festivals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Winter Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Tanabata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabata|qq=}} * {{ill|Community building|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Community building|qq=}} * {{ill|Homecoming|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homecoming|qq=}} * {{ill|Summer Vacation|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Vacation|qq=}}
*

<!-- The "Dashi Festival," held the day after Waraji Festival in October, features floats used for the "Ren Dashi" procession. However, as it is a ritual of Inari Shrine rather than Haguro Shrine, some citizens are opposed to holding it alongside the Waraji Festival. The Dashi Festival started as part of the 100th anniversary of Fukushima City's municipal government in 2007 and has continued since 2008 under different names such as "Dashi Festival," "Dashi Festa," and "Dashi Matsuri." (2021-01-19) --><!--Fukushima Waraji Festival--><!--Fukushima Waraji Festival--><!--Fukushima Waraji Festival--><!--Fukushima Waraji Festival--><!--Fukushima Waraji Festival--><!--Fukushima Waraji
